title: Scam
review:
Everyone — do not install this app! These are scammers! Under the guise of technical issues, they steal money.

When installing the app, it stated that the first week was a free trial and that we would be notified before the trial period ended and before any money was charged. However, after five days, I received a notification from my credit card that this app had attempted to charge my card, but I never received any email warning that the trial period was ending. Moreover, the charge they attempted was for a full annual subscription. Fortunately, my credit card declined the transaction.

I then went to the “Subscriptions” section in my phone’s settings, but this app was not listed there. After that, I logged into my account within the app’s settings and tried to cancel the subscription. I pressed the delete/cancel option multiple times, but the subscription would not be removed. I also tried to switch from the annual plan to a monthly plan, but that didn’t work either.

I decided not to do anything further at that moment, but the next morning they tried to charge my card again. I then went into the app and DELETED MY ACCOUNT. Two days later, they tried to charge my card again.

In the end, I had to block my card and request a replacement.

title: Amazing app!
review:
This is hands down the BEST app for accent training! I’ve tried many other apps and none of the others comes even close to the quality of BoldVoice! 
BoldVoice is very effective because it combines many tools to help you improve your accent. First, it scans your pronunciation and gives you a customized lesson plan targeting your specific needs. Then, every lesson starts with an instructional video how to pronounce the sounds you are having difficulty with. This is followed by a practice session - during the exercises you get immediate feedback on your pronunciation of all sounds of the word or sentence. 
It’s very effective and I’d say a little bit addictive 😄 I can hardly wait to my next session.

title: Racist app that keeps colonial mentality alive. There is no such a thing as American accent.
review: There is no single “American accent” — the U.S. has many accents. Teaching one as the standard reinforces a colonial and racist mindset, especially when people are bullied in Western countries based on how they look or sound. Ironically, accents are policed, but so are appearances — even Americans with accents have been harassed recently. Respectfully, passing often depends more on how you look than how you speak. Apps like this risk reinforcing that harmful hierarchy.

title: ⭐️ 1 star – Extremely misleading, charged $150 after cancellation
review:
This app is deceptive.

I signed up for the free trial, canceled it on time, and still got charged $150. Before the charge, I double-checked my Apple ID subscriptions and BoldVoice was NOT listed as an active subscription. I have screenshots proving this, yet the charge went through anyway.

This is not a mistake — this is a serious transparency issue. A free trial that turns into a hidden charge is unacceptable. If a subscription doesn’t even appear in Apple’s system, users have no way to protect themselves.

Now I’m forced to fight for a refund for a charge I never authorized. This feels predatory and incredibly unprofessional.

Do not trust this app. Use at your own risk.

title: Best for Fine Tuning Pronunciation
review: I have been using BoldVoice for 10 months now. I also subscribe to ELSA Speak and have tried other apps on trial basis. I use and like BoldVoice the most because it is the most sensitive in detecting mispronunciations. It also provides the most relevant and helpful solution to correct the mispronunciation. However, BoldVoice’s AI can be quite frustrating at times, especially after you’ve mispronounced a word. It has a tendency to keep saying that you are mispronouncing the same word even though you have made the correction.  BoldVoice allows you unlimited use so you can train more when you’re free. ELSA Speak allows limited training per day so you have to come back the next day once you have completed your daily training. ELSA Speak’s coach sounds robotic so it feels less natural but it’s AI conversation and feedback is very good. All these apps have their pros and cons so do your research and try different apps to find the right ones for you. They usually offer a free trial period. I am 55 years old. I am not trying to completely remove my Malaysian accent. I just want to sound clearer to Americans and BoldVoice fits the bill nicely.

title: Potential Scam and Fraudulent Billing Practices
review:
Warning: Potential Scam and Fraudulent Billing Practices

I would give zero rating since no option to give zero, giving one. 
I signed up for a 7-day trial and attempted to unsubscribe well within the trial period. The system repeatedly blocked cancellation and instead pushed a 50% “offer,” after which I was charged $99 without authorization. Shortly after, I was also charged $149 plus an additional $99, totaling charges for two accounts I never approved.

When I contacted customer service, I was told nothing could be done, despite clear evidence that the charges occurred during my attempt to cancel. My emails received automated responses with no resolution.

These practices appear deceitful, misleading, and potentially fraudulent. Consumers deserve transparency, fair billing, and the ability to cancel trials without being charged multiple unauthorized fees.

I urge Apple, Google Play, and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to review and investigate BoldVoice’s billing practices.
Please be cautious  in my experience, this service did not honor its trial, cancellation, or customer service commitments.

title: So far so good
review:
I’ve always struggled a bit with my English, especially my pronunciation. I can tell when people don’t fully understand me because of my accent. I learned English from a non-native teacher, so while I know the grammar and have a decent vocabulary, we didn’t really focus on speaking accurately. Every country also shapes sounds differently, which makes it even trickier.

I’m on my third day of the trial, and I have to say, this app is really helpful! I love that it’s interactive and shows you the tongue placement, so you can actually see and practice how to pronounce things correctly. I’ve already noticed mistakes I never realized I was making before (and honestly, only a couple of people ever pointed them out in the past ten years living in the USA).

I’ll definitely keep using it after the trial. It’s such a great tool, but like anything, it really depends on how determined and consistent you are. Rushing through just to get a checkmark or star won’t take you very far. Knowledge and skill come only with real practice.

Good luck with your studies, everyone!

title: Impressive for refining one’s accent, yet marred by a major limitation
review:
Bold Voice is, without question, an excellent app for refining one’s English pronunciation. It proves particularly beneficial for non-native speakers seeking to cultivate a more precise and confident accent. However, this commendable utility is overshadowed by its rather exorbitant monthly subscription fee, which, quite frankly, feels unjustifiable for many.

While I do appreciate the inclusion of a seven-day free trial, a generous gesture in theory. The pricing thereafter borders on avarice. One cannot help but question the necessity of such steep charges. A tool so helpful ought to be accessible, not placed behind such a prohibitive paywall.

title: Good but Spammy
review: Core content and lessons are great, but so much junk and notifications after each lesson. There’s almost as many spam screens to click through as content (“you’ve won a star”, “did you feel like you’ve leveled up?”, “challenge a friend”, “you hit a streak”, etc…). There’s 4-5 pages of nonsense to click through. A couple might be Ok but it’s almost drowning out the actual content now.

title: Great but still some rough edges
review:
I’ve been on an accent reduction journey for over 2 years and this app is an invaluable tool provided you have enough knowledge to take it with a grain of salt.

The AI can’t handle edge cases such as nasalizing an ‘aa’ sound before a nasal consonant, expecting you to say “thanks” in an awkward uncanny mix of British and American English. More frustratingly, there are lots of discrepancies between IPA transcriptions and expected pronunciations and support is unhelpful if you report those, asking you for a screenshot as if the report function couldn’t tell them which word was wrong.

title: very helpful for general purposes
review:
My dad, an immigrant, has been complaining about his accent since before I can remember. I decided to look up apps for him to try, and out of all of the ones J tried, this is definitely the most feature-rich. Keep in mind that I haven’t tried the paid version yet.

One drawback here is that this app is clueless when it comes to local accents. As a Coloradan, and more importantly, a Gen Z-er, I was surprised to find on the first test to find that I was barely straddling between Proficient and Fluent. I took it again, and barely got proficient. It flagged some really dumb stuff that most of the people I know here say also. For example, nobody says the “t” in “football”. And for some reason it kept flagging “is” and like words, even though “iz” is (no pun intended) how most people say it. 

So, overall great. Would love to work with the team on improving that. Good luck and have fun 😀

title: I was hesitating and then…
review: First I had to spend money in so many failed courses until I gave this app a try. I hope this app was available 24 years ago when I arrived in the U.S. So many lost opportunities because my English didn’t improve for two decades. I’m learning to use parts of my tongue that I didn’t know I could move. I feel tongue pain for all the times a repeat sounds that I didn’t know I could make with my mouth. And now I understand why gringos couldn’t understand 100% of what I said. If my tongue couldn’t produce almost 40 different sounds Americans use that are not in my maternal language, how is anyone going to understand me. The greatest features are the compare and record. I record myself sometimes 30 times until I finally understand what part of the word I have been pronouncing wrong. My tongue needs to hurt until it can get used to produce the new sound. Recorded video explanations show where to place each section of your tongue. And only a machine can help with that tedious process. No human being have that level of patience. Loved the free trial so much that I directly suscribed later to the most advanced version.
